center.name: 尤卡坦半岛
center.summary: 尤卡坦半岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联19种鸟类，包括Singing quail、眼斑吐綬雞、美洲紅鸛等19种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Belize, the Cayman Islands, Colombia (San Andrés island), Honduras (Bay Islands), Jamaica, and Mexico (Yucatán Peninsula)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: J.|title=The birds of El Salvador|year=1938|volume=23|publisher=Field Museum of Natural History|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/2779758#page/234/mode/1up}} They also adapt to cultivated land such as henequen and corn fields. Its breeding range is in southwestern Mexico, north into the western side of the Sierra Madre Occidental range, along with northern Central America and a disjunct range in the northern Yucatán Peninsula. It overlaps with the range of the greater roadrunner in a small area in the states of Sonora, Sinaloa, Jalisco, and Michoacán.{{cite book|last1=Howell|first1=Steve N

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The lovely cotinga is found in southern Mexico from southern Veracruz across northern Oaxaca and Chiapas south of the Yucatán Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The orange oriole (Icterus auratus) is a small bird species with orange feathers native to the Icteridae family in the Yucatán Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate rose-throated tanager is found in the northern, drier, part of Mexico's Yucatán Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rufous-necked wood rail is found in Mexico on the Pacific coast and the coast of the Yucatán Peninsula and generally (though discontinuously) along both the Pacific and Caribbean coasts of Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies is found in the east from Mexico's Yucatán Peninsula south through Belize and Guatemala into the western half of Honduras

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The singing quail is found in several separate areas in northern, western, and southern Mexico; the Yucatán Peninsula; northern Belize; much of Guatemala, and spottily in El Salvador and Honduras

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It also breeds from southern Nuevo León and southern Tamaulipas in northeastern Mexico south through eastern Mexico, including the Yucatán Peninsula, to join the western range in Guatemala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Yucatán Peninsula of Belize, Guatemala, and Mexico.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/ |title=IOC World Bird List (v 11.2) |last1=Gill |first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is sometimes referred to as the red-vented woodpecker. The Yucatan woodpecker is found in Belize and Mexico, and ranges over the entire Yucatán Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the Yucatán Peninsula where its habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forest, plantations and cleared areas at altitudes up to

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The wedge-tailed sabrewing is found in Mexico's Yucatán Peninsula and northeastern Chiapas, Belize, and northern Guatemala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Vaux's swift breeds in highlands from southern Alaska to central California and from southern Mexico, the northern Yucatán Peninsula, to eastern Panama and northern Venezuela

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: guatemalensis, from Veracruz, Chiapas, and the Yucatán Peninsula south through Belize, Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras, Nicaragua, and Costa Rica into western Panama The pale-billed woodpecker inhabits the interior and edges of a variety of forested landscapes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The other subspecies are found from southern Tamaulipas in northeastern Mexico south into the Yucatan Peninsula, Belize, and northern Guatemala, and (slightly separately) in northern Honduras. The white-bellied wren inhabits a range of woodland types from the semi-arid forest of western Mexico to the humid rainforest of the Yucatan Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The ocellated turkey (Meleagris ocellata) is a species of turkey residing primarily in the Yucatán Peninsula, Mexico, as well as in parts of Belize and Guatemala. A relative of the North American wild turkey (Meleagris gallopavo), it was sometimes previously considered in a genus of its own (Agriocharis), but the differences between the two turkeys are currently considered too small to justify generic segregation.{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EAVPDgAAQBAJ&pg=PA135 |page=135 |title=The Archaeology of Mesoamerican Animals |author1=Kitty F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits Central America from south-east Mexico (mostly the Yucatán Peninsula), to Costa Rica, where it is common and not considered threatened

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The American flamingo (Phoenicopterus ruber) is a large species of flamingo native to the West Indies, northern South America (including the Galápagos Islands) and the Yucatán Peninsula
